Book 2 — printed & available now

Stories that build calm, confident, intelligent thinkers.

A calm illustrated universe where Little Fox learns to feel, think, and choose. No villains. No winners. No lectures.

Meet Little Fox  → Join the Club

Hi, I’m Little Fox.

Nice to meet you. I don’t always get things right — but I think that’s the whole point. To learn. To grow.

How would you like to join me on my adventures?

🦊  Series 1  ·  Book 2

Little Fox Thinks It Through

Little Fox has an idea he feels sure about. But along the way, he discovers that others see things differently. What happens when we slow down long enough to listen, explain, and stay? A gentle story about thoughtfulness, perspective, and finding understanding together.

Little Fox Books

Pick your Little Fox

Books that build emotional literacy — shipped to your door.

Book 1

The Busy Day

Little Fox is having the kind of day where everything feels too much — too loud, too fast,…Little Fox is having the kind of day where everything feels too much — too loud, too fast, too full. But somewhere between the rushing and the doing, he finds a quiet moment. A gentle story about feelings, focus, and the power of simply stopping to breathe.

Book 2

Thinks It Through

Little Fox has an idea he feels sure about. But along the way, he discovers that others se…Little Fox has an idea he feels sure about. But along the way, he discovers that others see things differently. What happens when we slow down long enough to listen, explain, and stay? A gentle story about thoughtfulness, perspective, and finding understanding together.

Every Month

The Wild Challenge

Every Club box includes a Wild Challenge card — a nature task tied to that month's story. Pick up litter to keep Fox's forest safe. Find something living. Leave a place better than you found it.

Each month we choose a winner — a family whose challenge entry made us smile, think, or want to go outside. Winners are featured in our newsletter and receive an exclusive bonus character card not available anywhere else.

On Safeguarding

We never publish photos of children on our public website. Challenge entries — photos of nature, activities, and places — are shared only in our private subscriber community, with explicit consent. Your child's face stays private. Their actions speak for themselves.

Join to enter this month's challenge
This Month's Challenge
Find something in nature that doesn't belong there — and take it home.

Fox's forest stays beautiful because someone cared. Show us what you found, and what you did with it. Share using #WildChallenge

Last Month's Winner
The Okafor family
Collected 2kg of litter from their local park
For Educators & Communities

Little Fox in the real world

From classrooms to community groups, Little Fox is built to travel. Every setting gets a structured experience — not just a book.

Curriculum-ready

Plug-and-play PSHE — no prep required

Each session is completely self-contained. Open the box, read the book, use the guide. Works alongside PSHE, circle time, English, or as a morning calm session.

Curriculum-aligned discussion guides for every title
Reflection activity sheets — printable or projected
Digital audio tracks for whole-class listening
Teacher reading notes included — no specialist training needed
Get a free sample pack →
The Wild Day Out

Read together, then go outside

A complete half-day experience for libraries, parent groups, or school trips. The story opens the conversation — the outdoors continues it.

Read the book together as a group
Head outside for the Wild Challenge
Circle-time reflection using the activity sheet
Children write or draw their own Wild story to finish
Download the free guide →

Request a Free Sample Pack

We'll send Book 2 + a full discussion guide within 48 hours.

"

What happens when we slow down long enough to listen, explain, and stay?

🌱

Slow, not simplified

We don't rush to the lesson. Each story sits with the feeling first. Children need time to understand before they need answers.

🦊

No moral stated

Little Fox never tells you what to think. The story ends with an image, a silence, a question. The meaning belongs to the child.

🌿

A depth ladder

Books 1–5 explore single themes. Books 6–10 hold two at once. Books 11 onwards sit with conflict, nuance and true complexity.

📖

A 100-book universe

Five series, a locked art canon, a consistent world. Little Fox grows across every book — and so does the child reading alongside him.

Coming Soon

Little Fox for the Very Young

Simple, quiet picture books for ages 1–3. Little Fox is Happy. Little Fox is Sad. Big feelings, small words, beautiful illustrations.
